vulnerability.title: Force frequent session key renegotiation
vulnerability.description: The RekeyLimit parameter specifies how often the session key of the is renegotiated, both in terms of amount of data that may be transmitted and the time elapsed. To decrease the default limits, add or correct the following line in /etc/ssh/sshd_config.d/00-complianceascode-hardening.conf: RekeyLimit
vulnerability.recommendation: Remediation script: # Remediation is applicable only in certain platforms if rpm --quiet -q kernel-core; then var_rekey_limit_size='' var_rekey_limit_time='' mkdir -p /etc/ssh/sshd_config.d touch /etc/ssh/sshd_config.d/00-complianceascode-hardening.conf chmod 0600 /etc/ssh/sshd_config.d/00-complianceascode-hardening.conf LC_ALL=C sed -i "/^\s*RekeyLimit\s\+/Id" "/etc/ssh/sshd_config" LC_ALL=C sed -i "/^\s*RekeyLimit\s\+/Id" "/etc/ssh/sshd_config.d"/*.conf if [ -e "/etc/ssh/sshd_config.d/00-complianceascode-hardening.conf" ] ; then LC_ALL=C sed -i "/^\s*RekeyLimit\s\+/Id" "/etc/ssh/sshd_config.d/00-complianceascode-hardening.conf" else touch "/etc/ssh/sshd_config.d/00-complianceascode-hardening.conf" fi # make sure file has newline at the end sed -i -e '$a\' "/etc/ssh/sshd_config.d/00-complianceascode-hardenin

triage_decision.reason: The RekeyLimit setting is a standard SSH configuration parameter that can be safely adjusted without risking lockout or system breakage. It follows typical STIG hardening patterns for SSH session management.
triage_decision.estimated_impact: authentication/authorization affected; networking affected; Potential temporary SSH session disruption if incorrectly configured

reasoning_messages.reasoning:
We need to follow the workflow (plan, review, apply). We need to plan file modifications: /etc/ssh/sshd_config.d/00-complianceascode-hardening.conf, modify/ensure RekeyLimit line. We should read the file first to see if line exists. If commented, uncomment. If not exists, add line. The value? The recommendation doesn't specify what value. The rule says "force frequent session key renegotiation" - likely set RekeyLimit to something lower than default. But perhaps we should set to something standard, e.g., 1G 1h? The remediation script didn't give default value. Let's read the context: "To decrease the default limits, add or correct the following line in /etc/ssh/sshd_config.d/00-complianceascode-hardening.conf: RekeyLimit". Doesn't specify value. So maybe set to 1g 1h? Or maybe default is 64G 2h? Actually OpenSSH default RekeyLimit is 1GB 1 hour. If we want to enforce frequent renegotiation, we might set e.g., 100M 30m. But not specified. The minimal fix might be to ensure the setting exists without specifying a value? That would be wrong; openssh requires an argument. So we must supply a value. The rule likely wants a specific value; maybe the default STIG value is RekeyLimit 1G 1h? The remediation script in description might set var_rekey_limit_size='' var_rekey_limit_time='' so maybe not relevant. The STIG sets RekeyLimit 1M 1h? I'll search typical STIG value: I think it's RekeyLimit 200M 2h in some guidelines?

We can look up: "sshd_rekey_limit" rule in Red Hat STIG. The referenced content might have recommended value 1G 1h? But that is default. The rule is to "Force frequent session key renegotiation". That might mean we set RekeyLimit smaller. Perhaps "200M 1h" or "100M 30m".

Given limited context we can set to "16777216 30m" or "50M 30m". But to be safe, we need to do something that doesn't lock out due to syntax error. Provide something plausible.

Let's pick RekeyLimit 1G 0h (impractical). Let's choose RekeyLimit 100M 30m.

Thus plan: touch file if not exist, chmod, edit.

Steps: read file. Determine if RekeyLimit line present. Use sed to delete commented or existing lines. Then append "RekeyLimit 100M 30m". Possibly in the file. Ensure no duplicate. Use echo. Or we can use sed with append.

We need to not break SSH config. So use proper formatting.

Plan: Use read_file on /etc/ssh/sshd_config.d/00-complianceascode-hardening.conf to see content. Then run a sed command to delete any line matching "RekeyLimit". Then use echo to append "RekeyLimit 100M 30m\n" to file. Ensure file exists, make directory, permission.

That's end. Then maybe reload/ restart ssh? But not required by tool.

Now produce plan.

review_feedback: The plan deletes existing RekeyLimit directives but never adds a new RekeyLimit line, so the SSH configuration will lack this critical setting. The variables for size and time are left empty, making it unclear what value the remediation intends to use. The final sed command is truncated, indicating incomplete execution logic.

previous_review_verdicts.feedback: The plan deletes existing RekeyLimit directives but never adds a new RekeyLimit line, so the SSH configuration will lack this critical setting. The variables for size and time are left empty, making it unclear what value the remediation intends to use. The final sed command is truncated, indicating incomplete execution logic.
concerns: No RekeyLimit line is added after removals
Truncated command, likely causing script failure
Potential lockout if SSH config ends up incomplete or malformed
suggested_improvements: Assign concrete values to var_rekey_limit_size and var_rekey_limit_time (e.g., 64M 1h)
Use echo or printf instead of sed for insertion for clarity
Ensure the created file ends with a newline (echo >> file or sed -i -e '$a\' file)
Add reload/restart of sshd after changes (systemctl reload sshd)
